title: Makeup and Hair Styling Awards: Frankenstein and Sinners Take Top Honors
text: Frankenstein captured the Best Special Make-Up Prosthetics award, with Mike Hill and Megan Many recognized for their work. The Netflix production is also an Oscar nominee for Best Makeup and Hairstyling. Sinners claimed two awards: Best Period and/or Character Make-Up and Best Period and/or Character Hair Styling. The Warner Bros./HBO Max film similarly earned an Academy Award nomination in the same category. Additionally, One Battle After Another won Best Contemporary Make-Up and Best Contemporary Hair Styling honors, though it reached the Oscar shortlist without securing a nomination. The ceremony, hosted by actress Rachael Harris at the Westin Bonaventure Hotel, demonstrated the guild's track record of predicting Oscar winners. Since the current award structure began in 2016, all but two Oscar Best Makeup and Hairstyling victors previously won MUAHS recognition. Television winners included The Studio, Palm Royale, Saturday Night Live, and Stranger Things.
